calendar_static_analysis.crud_correctness: PASS — insertEvent/updateEvent/deleteEvent paths in src/lib/sync.js correctly map camelCase->snake_case; saveEventWithChildren handles M:N via upsert + delete-then-insert on events_children
calendar_static_analysis.isolation: PASS — visibleEvents filter uses family_members.id (not auth.uid); parent uses selectedChild.id; child uses myFamilyMemberId; is_family_event=true bypasses both
calendar_static_analysis.optimistic_update: PASS for edit branch (snapshot + revert on catch); PARTIAL for insert branch (no rollback, relies on 30s poll to clean up)
calendar_static_analysis.realtime_catch_join: PASS — fetchEventById re-fetches events_children join on every INSERT/UPDATE postgres_changes notification
calendar_static_analysis.month_indexing: 0-indexed consistently across insert/edit/filter/sticker/memo; dateLabel uses +1 for display
calendar_static_analysis.recurrence: weekly repeat generates N independent events (no RRULE) — intentional per product scope
concerns: F1 P1: push body for edit_event renders 0-indexed month verbatim (2026-04-13 means May 13 to user) — App.jsx:4089
F2 P1: saveEventWithChildren is 3 sequential REST calls without transaction — partial failure desyncs events_children
insert-branch optimistic update has no rollback path (eventually corrected by 30s poll)

memo_static_analysis.realtime_setup: PASS — memo_replies postgres_changes channel + broadcast memo_reply fallback both wired; 1s reconciler polls while memo page open
memo_static_analysis.read_state: PASS — mark_memo_reply_read RPC (SECURITY DEFINER, atomic append) with read-modify-write fallback; IntersectionObserver triggers after 3s continuous visibility
memo_static_analysis.multichild_isolation: PASS — server-side .eq(child_id) filter + client-side isMemoForSelectedChild policy B (NULL ignored)
memo_static_analysis.self_echo: INSERT handler drops on newRow.user_id === authUser.id — assumes one device per user; multi-device same-user (co-parent on phone+tablet) misses realtime, relies on 1s poll
concerns: F3 P2: 30s polling fallback does not include memo_replies — memo-page-closed + realtime drop = stale until page reopens
F6 P2: self-user INSERT skip drops memos for multi-device same-user scenarios
F7 P3: cross-timezone parent/child (parent KST, child EST) may see dateKey mismatch — by design ko-KR product

polling_fallback_coverage.verdict: PASS — gaps acceptable per current UX (background staleness self-heals on page open)

issues.title: Push body shows wrong month (off-by-one) on edit_event
issues.description: App.jsx:4089 builds push message body by replacing - with / in messageDateKey. messageDateKey month is 0-indexed (currentMonth = today.getMonth()) so May 13 renders as 2026/4/13. Saved data unaffected; user-facing notification copy mislabels month.
issues.recommended_fix: Split messageDateKey by '-', render as ${y}/${m+1}/${d} or ${y}년 ${m+1}월 ${d}일. Audit any other path consuming raw dateKey for display.

issues.title: saveEventWithChildren is non-atomic; partial failure desyncs events_children
issues.description: Sequence is (1) upsert events, (2) delete all events_children for event_id, (3) insert new events_children rows. Each call is a separate REST request with no transaction wrapper. If step 3 fails (network glitch, RLS rejection), step 1+2 commit and the event ends up with no children — disappears from every child's filter because is_family_event=false and child_ids=[].
issues.recommended_fix: Wrap in a SECURITY DEFINER RPC (single transaction). Alternative: fall back to is_family_event=true when childIds.length === 0 to avoid the 'invisible event' failure mode.

issues.title: 30s polling fallback omits memo_replies
issues.description: App.jsx:2412 30-second poll covers events + memos + saved_places. memo_replies relies on the 1s memo-page-open poll only. Memo page closed + realtime drop = stale until user opens memo page.
issues.recommended_fix: Add a quiet fetchMemoRepliesForDateKeys call for the current dateKey window in the 30s loop. Low cost.

issues.title: events_children INSERT policy lacks family-scope check on child_id
issues.description: RLS WITH CHECK validates event_id is in caller's family events, but does not validate child_id is in caller's family_members. A misbehaving authenticated client with primary parent role could insert (event_id, foreign_child_id). No known attack vector via current UX (selectedChild is family-scoped) but defense-in-depth gap.
issues.recommended_fix: Extend WITH CHECK to require child_id IN (SELECT id FROM family_members WHERE family_id = (SELECT family_id FROM events WHERE id = event_id)).

issues.title: GRANT ALL ON daily_supplies TO anon
issues.description: Grant exposes all DML to anon. RLS blocks because auth.uid() is null, but the grant is broader than other tables (which use GRANT TO authenticated only). Inconsistent + raises attack surface.
issues.recommended_fix: REVOKE ALL ON daily_supplies FROM anon; GRANT SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE ON daily_supplies TO authenticated;

issues.title: Self-user INSERT skip drops memos for multi-device same-user
issues.description: onMemoRepliesChange handler skips when newRow.user_id === authUser.id to prevent optimistic double-render. Same user on two devices (e.g. co-parent on phone + tablet) means device B misses realtime; only the 1s memo-page poll recovers.
issues.recommended_fix: Replace self-skip with device-id check (sender_device_id in broadcast payload) OR rely on the existing id dedup at line 2009 to handle optimistic state without needing self-skip.

issues.title: Cross-timezone parent/child see dateKey mismatch
issues.description: dateKey is device-local (today.getMonth()/getDate()). Parent in KST and child in EST will compute different dateKey for same UTC moment near midnight. Memo for 2026-05-12 on child side may not match parent's 2026-05-13.
issues.recommended_fix: Out-of-scope for v1.0 (Asia/Seoul product). For v1.1+: standardize on Asia/Seoul-derived dateKey via Intl.DateTimeFormat.

summary_line: STATUS=PARTIAL_VERIFIED | P0=0 P1=2 | calendar_static=PASS memo_static=PASS publications=10/10 OK
